author: Tertullian
book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Grant that Beauty Be Not to Be Feared: Still It is to Be Shunned as Unnecessary and Vainglorious.

ccel.title: ANF04. Fathers of the Third Century: Tertullian, Part Fourth; Minucius Felix; Commodian; Origen, Parts First and Second

source.title: ANF04. Fathers of the Third Century: Tertullian, Part Fourth; Minucius Felix; Commodian; Origen, Parts First and Second

title: Grant that Beauty Be Not to Be Feared: Still It is to Be Shunned as Unnecessary and Vainglorious.

source.title: ANF04. Fathers of the Third Century: Tertullian, Part Fourth; Minucius Felix; Commodian; Origen, Parts First and Second

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: Let it now be granted that excellence of form be not to be feared, as neither troublesome to its possessors, nor destructive to its desirers, nor perilous to its compartners; let it be thought (to be) not exposed to temptations, not surrounded by stumbling-blocks: it is enough that to angels of God it is not necessary.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: For, where modesty is, there beauty is idle; because properly the use and fruit of beauty is voluptuousness, unless any one thinks that there is some other harvest for bodily grace to reap.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: Are women who think that, in furnishing to their neighbour that which is demanded of beauty, they are furnishing it to themselves also, to augment that (beauty) when (naturally) given them, and to strive after it when not (thus) given?

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: Some one will say, “Why, then, if voluptuousness be shut out and chastity let in, may (we) not enjoy the praise of beauty alone, and glory in a bodily good?”

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: Let whoever finds pleasure in “glorying in the flesh” see to that.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: To us in the first place, there is no studious pursuit of “glory,” because “glory” is the essence of exaltation.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: Now exaltation is incongruous for professors of humility according to God’s precepts.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: Secondly, if all “glory” is “vain” and insensate, how much more (glory) in the flesh, especially to us?

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: For even if “glorying” is (allowable), we ought to wish our sphere of pleasing to lie in the graces of the Spirit, not in the flesh; because we are “suitors” of things spiritual.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: In those things wherein our sphere of labour lies, let our joy lie.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: From the sources whence we hope for salvation, let us cull our “glory.”

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: Plainly, a Christian will “glory” even in the flesh; but (it will be) when it has endured laceration for Christ’s sake, in order that the spirit may be crowned in it, not in order that it may draw the eyes and sighs of youths after it.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: Thus (a thing) which, from whatever point you look at it, is in your case superfluous, you may justly disdain if you have it not, and neglect if you have.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: Let a holy woman, if naturally beautiful, give none so great occasion (for carnal appetite).

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: Certainly, if even she be so, she ought not to set off (her beauty), but even to obscure it.
